entities.name: BRADYRHIZOBIUM SP. (ARACHIS) STRAIN NC92
entities.description: A member of the cowpea cross-inoculation group used for mutagenesis to study symbiotic phenotypes.

entities.name: RANDOM TRANSPOSON TNS MUTAGENESIS
entities.description: A technique used to create mutants of Bradyrhizobium sp. (Arachis) strain NC92.

entities.name: COWPEA CROSS-INOCULATION GROUP
entities.description: The taxonomic or functional group to which Bradyrhizobium sp. (Arachis) strain NC92 belongs.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A host plant used to test the symbiotic phenotype of Bradyrhizobium mutants.

entities.name: SIRATRO
entities.description: A host plant used to test the symbiotic phenotype of Bradyrhizobium mutants.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A host plant used to test the symbiotic phenotype of Bradyrhizobium mutants.

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The ability of the bacteria to form nodules on host plants, often referred to as Nod phenotype.

entities.name: NODABCD
entities.description: Common nodulation genes involved in the process of nodule formation.

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia, often referred to as Fix phenotype.

entities.name: ACETYLENE REDUCTION ACTIVITY
entities.description: A biochemical assay used to measure the activity of the enzyme nitrogenase.

entities.name: NITROGENASE
entities.description: The enzyme complex responsible for the reduction of nitrogen to ammonia.

entities.name: HOST-SPECIFIC FIXATION
entities.description: A phenotype where nitrogen fixation is defective on specific host plants while remaining effective on others.
